title: Callianthe fluviatilis
intro: Callianthe fluviatilis, also known as the water marigold, is a tropical plant species belonging to the family Malvaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which can aid in wound healing and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Callianthe fluviatilis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Callianthe fluviatilis is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments for skin conditions and wounds.
taxonomy: Callianthe fluviatilis is a species of flowering plant in the family Malvaceae. It is native to South America and is also known as the 'River Mallow'. This plant is classified under the family Malvaceae which includes other plants like hibiscus and hollyhock. The species Callianthe fluviatilis is further classified into various sub-species based on its geographical distribution and morphological characteristics. The classification of Callianthe fluviatilis is a subject of ongoing research and study in the field of botany.
